seoTitle: Johanna vs Johnsonville — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Johanna and Johnsonville. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Johanna and Johnsonville.
heading: Johanna vs Johnsonville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Johanna, Victoria and Johnsonville, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Johnsonville has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Johanna (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Johanna or Johnsonville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Johanna has a flood risk score of 60/100, while Johnsonville scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Johanna scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Johnsonville scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Johanna or Johnsonville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Johnsonville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
